items.q: Which relative pronoun do you use for 'the man WHOM I saw' in German?
items.why: Students don't decline relative pronouns. The relative pronoun takes the case its FUNCTION REQUIRES in its clause. 'Whom I saw' makes it the direct object, so accusative: 'Der Mann, DEN ich gesehen habe.'

items.misconception: Three separate words run together by mistake.
items.random_wrong: It is too long to be a real word.
items.why: Students think long words are typos. German freely COMPOUNDS nouns into single words: Donau + Dampf + Schiff + Fahrt + Gesellschaft. The last element determines the gender and the core meaning.
Students use English -s plural. German has multiple plural patterns including UMLAUT + er for many masculine nouns: Mann to Männer, Buch to Bücher, Land to Länder. Plurals must often be memorised.
It's Friedrich Merz. “Olaf Scholz” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Which party is the historic centre-left party?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: SPD. “CDU” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was the SPD founded (as the ADAV/SDAP predecessor)?
items.why: The real figure is 1863. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
